---
title: "Introduction"
author: "Truong Phi"
---

## Overview

The **Positional Burrows-Wheeler Transform (PBWT)**, introduced by Richard Durbin in 2014 [@durbin2014efficient], revolutionized how we handle massive genomic datasets. By applying the principles of the Burrows-Wheeler Transform to positional genetic data, it enables ultra-efficient haplotype matching and search operations that were previously computationally prohibitive.

The PBWT provides an efficient way to sort haplotypes at every position along a chromosome, enabling very fast identification of identical-by-descent (IBD) segments and other types of haplotype matching.

### Purposes


### Content covered



---

::: {#refs}
:::
